Called way-faring men —Jdj 19:17;Isa 35:8.
Preparations made by, alluded to —Eze 12:3,4.
Often collected together and formed caravans —Ge 37:25;Isa 21:13;Lu 2:44.
Often engaged persons acquainted with the country as guides —Nu 10:31,32;Job 29:15.
Friends of
  Often supplied them with provision. —Ge 21:14;44:1;Jer 40:5.
  Sometimes accompanied them a short way. —2Sa 19:31;Ac 20:38;21:5.
  Frequently commended them to protection of God. —Ge 43:13,14;Ac 21:5.
  Frequently took leave of them with sorrow. —Ac 20:37;21:6.
  Often sent them away with music. —Ge 31:27.
Generally commenced their journey early in the morning —Jdj 19:5.
Generally rested at noon —Ge 18:1,3;Joh 4:6.
Halted at even —Ge 24:11.
Generally halted at wells or streams —Ge 24:11;32:21,23;Ex 15:27;1Sa 30:21;Joh 4:6.
Carried with them
  Provisions for the way. —Jos 9:11,12;Jdj 19:19.
  Provender for their beasts of burden. —Ge 42:27;Jdj 19:19.
  Skins filled with water, wine, &c. —Ge 21:14,15;Jos 9:13.
